channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: -5.2 billion dollars after 6 years of DCA — 38 replies — 152.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
-5.2 billion dollars after 6 years of DCA

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-03-28 22:21 UTC

OP

this nigga is coping hard

Early replies (first 3)

62040721
>REFINANCE, WHERE MICHAEL?

>DDDD

62040752
>>62038894
Mstr will be at 4k in 5 years and you will eat shit nigger

62040776
>>62038894
ITS ALL PART OF THE PLAN YOU WOULDNT GET IT

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62041459
>>62041421
2 retards dont make a right

62041479
>>62038894
how do i give this guy my money

62041552
>>62041479
You convinced me, I wanna know too

62041934
Some other anon in /smg/ told me they're aiming for a 11% dividend yield or something, but all i see is a CEO that post cringe tweets. Must be tough bagholding this

62042057
>>62041421
Lmao

62042072
>>62041934
Extremely bullish post

62042260
>>62041934
Where the fuck would the money even come from?

62042308
>>62038894
He is still up billions with his Micro Strategy Stock

62042319
>>62042260
bitcoin is going to go up because of the rainbow chart and because its GOING TO GO UP BECAUSE THERE'S ONLY HECKIN 21M BITCUNTS OUT THERE

62043624
>>62040752
Saylor, what are you doing here?

62043627
>>62040752
well said. it sucks dealing with all these sub 130 IQ cattle. imagine unironically shilling for the dollar and jewish banking system lmao

62043648
Whenever I see somebody wearing their own merch I immediately short their stock

62043658
>>62043648
Thank you for your contribution. I look forward to liquidating you

62043660
>>62041934
I told you that. And they aren’t “aiming for it”, they’re doing it. Pop in the /quant/ thread and I’ll sperg out for you.

Do you realize that MSTR was the #1 most profitable company in the US for awhile? They were making something like $500m per day selling Bitcoin volatility through their products.

If you actually look into the capital/debt structure you’ll see they’re in a stellar position. If you want to be long BTC, MSTR is just the better choice. It’s an engine that accretes BTC, and they’re going to pivot to banking at some point.

62043666
>>62042260
They already have it. If you don’t know how MSTR makes money, maybe you should research it?

62043720
>>62038894
Kek

62043746
>>62038894
The problem is that this retard doesn't DCA. He keeps buying local tops in order to keep the price up. He barely averages down. Why didn't he buy at 60k when he recently had a chance? He waited like a retard for it to recover.
>>62041934
>>62043660
NEOS' BTCI gets you more than double that. They also have NEHI (Ethereum) which yields 35% kek.

62043754
>>62038894
tom lee thinks those are rookie numbers

62043767
>>62043746
>an illiquid leveraged ETF is the same as a liquid BTC accretion engine
itsallsotiresome.jpg

Also, they buy tops because that's when volatility is highest and they have the most premium/leverage to strip. Please take your IQ takes to /r/buttcoin

62043788
>>62043767
/r/iamverysmart
And what happens to MSTR if Bitcoin goes sup 30k for 3 years?

62043861
his ponzi thing was only above par and buying bitcoin for a couple days this entire month which means he has to pay everyone out of his cash reserves

62043863
>>62043788
>And what happens to MSTR if Bitcoin goes sup 30k for 3 years?
Literally nothing, they could half that price for twice as long at current reserves and obligations. Not hard to figure this shit out yourself.

62043993
>>62038894
Michael Saylor is retarded. Even Epstein called him a retard. It seems obvious to anyone who isn't a retard that this guy isn't worth the attention.

62044254
>>62043627
Bitcoin is a jewish ponzi scheme and they want you to buy it instead of land, gold or guns

62044260
>>62038894
Just short when Gaylord buys.
Go all in when he gets liquidated.

Simple as

62045094
>>62043767
How is it illiquid or leveraged you peak dunning kruger ape? Holy cringe.

62045912
>>62043863
>you could half that price
Halve*
They’re hiring pajeets to fight fud now

item.title: If you visit the fast food restauraunt I work at, especially during the weekend, I hate you. — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you visit the fast food restauraunt I work at, especially during the weekend, I hate you.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-28 22:18 UTC

OP

If you visit the fast food restauraunt I work at, especially during the weekend, I hate you. That's the secret about all fast food employees. We fucking hate you. Immediately upon seeing your face, we are thinking nasty thoughts about you. Nepo baby. Nigger. Bourgeois. Street shitter. HR roastie. MIGAt. We fucking hate you. The smile is a lie.

Early replies (first 3)

62045752
>>62045737
I feel sorry for all you but it is what it is, it's not my fault you ended up doing a shitty job and you don't have the balls to quit. Of you want to find someone to really hate maybe you should look yourself in the mirror

62045762
thats just you, youre a sociopath

62045859
Poorfags in service work are why I hardly ever eat out anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62045872
How much bodily fluid goes into the food on a typical day?

62045905
Spare me this gay little diatribe and put the fries in the bag bro

62045907
>>62045737
I never order in person. I am nothing but a number on a screen to you. You are nothing at all to me.

62045908
i assume this for everyone

62045910
>>62045907
doordashGOD...

item.title: Why do you invest? Why do you like to get more money? Why do you pushyv — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do you invest? Why do you like to get more money? Why do you pushyv

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-03-28 22:28 UTC

OP

For me, as an incel in my 30s, I get literally zero female attention.
My biggest dream was to have a family, but despite all my efforts, I can’t even get friendzoned by a woman, let alone date.
I only live and try to make money, because I believe that in a few decades or so, we will reach such a height (pun intended) of technology that it can put me out of my inceldom.
The AI we have today would have been predicted 10 years ago to come out around 2030-something, yet we have it today.
It has already solved protein folding issues that required decades of research.
There is a company that is using AI to find cancer DNA fragments in blood, which means, if successful, cancer can be tested just like vitamin D deficiency.
There is another company that was able to de-age lab animals by 50% and got FDA approval for the first human trials.
All this is going to cost money.
That’s why I save a lot of money.
If I die before that, eh. It’s not like I was even able to live the way I want to.
But if I live and see that age, maybe I can pay for these and many others and hopefully get out of my inceldom once and for all.
What are your goals?

Early replies (first 3)

62045839
>>62045799
Sure thing, anon. Scientists are working 24/7 to make incels happy. You keep saving and waiting

62045929
>>62045839
Yeah? I mean scientists work because they love science and they want a pay check doing it.
It’s the financier job to market those discoveries and as long as people exists, they will be able to market.
This is my belief that in a few decade, based on current tech trajectory and with AI help, we can achieve those things, and I am pretty sure it would be super expensive and definitely subscription based.
But I am not here to argue, I wanna know what’s your goal, your vision. Why do you push to make more money? Or maybe you don’t and you are just comfortable as you are.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 198 replies — 82.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 198 • Updated: 2026-03-28 20:09 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62034101
>>62033138
Or wait. Let's try this. Might be an interesting conversation. How so? What have I done or said to make you believe that?

62034381
7,5$ top in 2028
+400% from ATL

62034423
>>62032967
This exercise is very elementary.

>And not saying to believe my Bullshit
Do you live in a bubble purposely living in blissful ignorance to the outside world so you can be content?

62034490
>>62034423
>Do you live in a bubble purposely living in blissful ignorance to the outside world so you can be content?
How so? What makes you think that?

62034856
>>62034423
>This exercise is very elementary.
So, what you're saying is that it's easy to do and therefore it's appropriate? And that it gets the point across and makes the person doing the experiment have the necessary experience in order to become aware of how the mind works? That's great!

62034890
>>62034490
Reread your posts
>>62034856
Maybe for a few. Better than none I guess

62034963
>>62034890
Right... So your point is?

62035196
>>62034381
grim

62035839
>>62034963
I didn't have one

62036344
>>62035839
Ok. So, ... You wanna talk about anything? Anything on your mind?

You said
>Maybe for a few. Better than none I guess
What would you like to know about the mind, if anything? If you don't think the exercise is appropriate then it's because you already understand, right? And if you tell me what you don't understand then we can start moving in that direction, right? Or don't tell me. It's up to you

62037187
>>62034101
>178 replies
>105 pbtid

oh I dunno

62037296
>>62037187
Gavin is paying me to keep the thread alive. This is how we live in Mumbai my good sir. We shill coins while talking about the mind.

62037323
>>62037187
I need rupees to worship the Vishnu on the blockchain my good sar. And to feed my village of Mumbai, the city of DOT.

62037509
>>62037296
>>62037323

Post hand

62039073
>>62037509
I already did here >>62008295. True Mumbai warrior hand. My fathers name was Kumar, my mothers name was Lakshmi. Pic related. I'm the little prince

62039085
>>62037509
I am now a Vishnu warrior of the blockchain, living in the city of DOT, Mumbai. I run a scam call center, they call me papa Rupee over here. I make many Rupees for many villages. We feed whole of city of DOT, Mumbai. We eat very good. Our bellies are very full.

62040207
>>62039073
>>62039085

Shitposting or on hard drugs constantly?

62040932
>>62040207
I don't get the question? Help me understand what you mean. And thanks for bumping the thread. Gavin Wood, our lord and saviour, will be very pleased with you.

May the blockchain Vishnu be with you good sar

62041893
>>62040932
Read again slowly

62042472
>>62041893
Yea..... You're asking me if I'm shitposting or on hard drugs constantly? That's your question? I can't decipher your half-formed sentences to be honest.

62043369
>>62042472
Simple yes or no

62044274
>>62043369
I can't answer if you can't formulate and define what the question is, right? So, please define the question and then I can answer it

62044411
i'm very sad for this investment

62044561
>>62044411
That sounds really tough my fren. Siddartha Gautama said "Attachment is the root cause of all suffering.". Just practice detachment and sooner or later you'll be able to accept your loss. Maybe even grief it if it's a big loss

62044605
>>62044411
May the Vishnu on the blockchain be with you

62044783
>>62044411
Another thing Siddartha Gautama said is that the only two things you're entitled two in life is suffering and death.

62045355
>>62044561
>>62044605
>>62044783

Not very encouraging to attract new buyers

62045388
>>62045355
Ah. How so?

62045442
>>62045388
Perhaps share influential news

We know DOT is a game changer in the Web world.

62045475
>>62045442
>Perhaps share influential news
Like what?

item.title: baggies BTFO — 29 replies — 54.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
baggies BTFO

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-03-28 22:01 UTC

OP

baggies BTFO

Early replies (first 3)

62044277
>>62044256
biased selection, we're at july 2022 retard

62044281
>>62044256
LIKE BUYING BITCOIN IN 2021!

62044293
>>62044277
this guy fucks

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62044296
>>62044256
Are you stupid? We are already past the arrow. We are nearing the bottom by now.

62044297
Bitcoins are still on sale, sisters! Get them while they're cheap!

62044299
>>62044256
hes correct giga dump is incoming
2 more weeks

62044301
>>62044277
this, the nigger should put the second arrow 1 point higher..... fuck this nigger op and his whole bloodline!

62044328
>>62044277
Obviously this. We just crab for a while now. Maybe a slight capitulation dump to 40k when stocks tumble and liquidity dries up.

62044338
>>62044296
Seems like you can't read a chart brother. It's painfully obvious the bottom will be 40k.

62044349
>>62044299
2 more weeks until the super cycle starts!!!!

62044368
>>62044338
40k is near 60k
Delusional bobos think we're hitting lower lows out of nowhere.

62044370
>>62044368
nigga learn how to maths, thats a -30% drop

62044379
>>62044349
stop quoting cancers of crypto pls!

62044381
>>62044256

62044386
>>62044381
we are in january 2022

if trump keep shitting in the jam bitcoin could go to 30k

40k is 100% sure

62044431
>>62044386
trump is a nonrelevant nigger, DEATH TO SATAN!

62044440
Attached is the non-retard analysis.See you at 30k.

62044507
>>62044440
these are the dumbest meme lines I have ever seen

62044536
>>62044256
you are comparing a 45% rally with a 25% rally
there was no bulltrap yet and weekly rsi is too low, so if it drops below 60k it will bulldiv across multiple HTFs
needs longer consolidation or stronger bounce

62044559
>>62044256
how about this?

62044614
>>62044297
Waiting for the 67.2 snap, otherwise it's just gonna crab

62045615
>>62044614
American market isn't biting for some fucking reason. Crabbing it its.

62045624
>>62044256
I have so much dry powder to ape in with I really hope it crashes to 30k range but literally everyone is expecting prices to go down and are extra bearish because of the war which is why I am starting my DCA now

62045735
>>62045624
It's not going to go below 60k. There's been multiple attempts to force it down to 50, all have failed.

62045755
>>62044381
This is probably correct. All retards waiting for 30k are going to miss the bottom (again)

62045757
>>62044277
Why would anyone buy Bitcoin again after all the drops?

62045781
>>62044256
>Bobo can't into TA
someone teach them PLEASE

62045808
That is all

62045863
>>62044256

item.title: RTO is being pushed at my job. 2 days a week in a city relatively far from me (2 hour commute). Should I rent a small st… — 36 replies — 33.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
RTO is being pushed at my job. 2 days a week in a city relatively far from me (2 hour commute). Should I rent a small st…

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-03-28 21:28 UTC

OP

RTO is being pushed at my job. 2 days a week in a city relatively far from me (2 hour commute).
Should I rent a small studio apartment or should I take the bus? I'd have to waste about 13% of my wage (more for a bigger one).

Early replies (first 3)

62042819
>>62042805
I quit when they rtod 5 days a week with nothing lined up planning to do a year sabbatical. I had a recruiter already reach out and I am barely halfway through my sabbatical. Not sure why I am even following through

62042861
>>62042819
I could quit today if I wanted to, but I'd have to take a much lower pay, like a 30% cut or more if I got another job. I get messages from recruiters all the time, and I do apply from time to time, but so far no one has hired me, and they are all RTOing anyway...

62042866
>>62042805
Try the bus but I can almost guarantee that you you won't be able to stand it unless (desu even if) you can fall asleep easily on it and/or have a good way to occupy your time.

Keep in mind that finding a place takes its own commute. If you can dip from work early to attend a showing, that saves you the time and money of riding/driving in on your day off.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62042973
>>62042902
>But then again I'm critical personnel at this job and they can't fire me over this.
my role is kinda critical, but my boss is a dick sucking retard, so instead of using this to our advantage, the faggot keeps giving in to his boss, and has fired a bunch of coworkers. this is also a reason why I'm wary of renting. I could end up wasting a ton of money if they fire me.

62043107
>>62042805
We've RTO'd a dozen times but I'm still WFH 5 days a week and the office is still empty.

62043571
>>62043107
I should have done that instead of giving in, I guess. too bad I already said I would respect it...

62043682
>>62042805
Tell them you're not gonna do that shit at all and dare them to fire you.

62043730
>>62042805
Are the 2 days adjacent? If so, you can rent with roommates and only stay for a night, or two nights if the commute is much shorter during the night.
I work 2 days a week non-adjacent, in a city that is 90 minutes away from me, and that's after peak rush hour (I arrive before 10). When I started here, I used the bus for a few months. It takes 2.5 hours in the morning and 2 hours at night, but I liked it because I can do stuff while being driven. The problem though is the people. Imagine what kind of people are so lowly that they can't afford a car or a city apartment, those are the people you will meet in an inter-city bus. I met absolutely deranged schizos, drug users, and one time someone behind me started watching porn on his phone with the speakers on. In the winter it is too fucking hot because they always cater to the elderly and women. If a perfumed cunt or a heavy smoker boards then you just have to suffer it.
Also it just takes too much time. I lose 4.5 to 5 hours of my time on this shit, vs 3 hours when using a car, so I switched to a car. I hate the car as well but I'm just not going to be a rentcuck. Imagine paying out of your net income to cover some boomer's retirement or mortgage.
>>62043682
I wouldn't do this in this clown economy.

62043836
>>62042805
They are about to do energy lockdowns so the RTO will be cancelled

62043920
So what's the actual answer: why do they want people in the office when everything can get done from home fine?

62044124
>>62042805
this is my exact scenario for a year now, but the commute is ~2.5h depending on traffic. I get there by car, park for free not far from city center, then stay in a hotel overnight. I take the cheapest hotel that has private bathroom and is clean. Its not the best money wise but more convenient for me. i take the evening going to bars, playing pool or sightseeing, whatever. It wouldnt be worth it with 3 days in office tho.

62044200
>>62042805
They gave you the heads up to quit. Start looking now for a new job. Play hardball too fuck em and when you get a new job do what I do. I don’t give 2weeks notice I just box my stuff mail it with a note and never say shit again. FUCK EM. They wanna play. Play back.

>I have no car and no intention to get one

62044204
>>62043920
Who cares it’s a psychological game with these fucking freaks. Get a new job. Send your shit in a box with a photocopied picture of your dick and balls with a note saying “here’s my return to office” no 2 weeks sucker punch em back

62044205
>>62043730
All of this bullshit for fucking pay that’s not even 250-400k lmao I would never. I’d sooner be homeless man

62044622
>>62043920
everyone knows people are slacking off at home and only do the bare minimum. thats fine and you cant expect more than that from a regular employee desu but some retards go to far with it and pretty much do zero work which fucks up the deal for everyone else.

62044655
>>62042902
>hurr durr im so special and critical
They could replace you in a week and they’d murder your entire family in front of you to make more profit.

62044695
>>62044622
The people that slack off at home are the same people that slack off in the office.

62044716
>>62044695
thats true but these are usually the first ones who quit once they have to go back to office. seen that more than enough in my work place.

62044722
>>62043682
This is the only answer, all the other wagies that gave up on WFH and willingly returned to the office deserve the humiliation they get.

62044741
>Work for Nordic company
>CEO decides 3 day RTO
>They talk highly about the benefits of going to the office, after bragging about the benefits of WFH during covid
>They started tracking units first on adherence
>Now they are tracking individually
>Your boss follows this up and you even have an app to plan and track it yourself
>At the same time we are cutting costs, so traveling is highly restricted
>This especially goes out on those working in cross border teams
>Some have no team in their office, so they come to the office, punch in their card and go home to work again
>People are pissed and calling out the bullshit and how we are treated like kids
>Now most meetings are about this topic
>Probably millions are being wasted on this

Honestly what a shitshow.

62044775
I simply quit the day I'm told to get back into an office.
Simple as fucking that.

62044791
You WFH fags did this to yourselves. It wasn't 1-2% of people during COVID, it was 30-40% of you dickheads that were actively fucking off at home FOR FUCKING YEARS. You fags got years at home to fuck off. People abused bennies, WFH, COVIDbucks, stimmies, and now the pendulum is swinging back. Get fucked lol. All the people that worked jobs that never allowed for WFH are laughing at you dipshits right now, just like how you were laughing at wagies during COVID while you sat at home in your PJs the entire fucking day.

t. wagie who never got a single stimmie check and never did WFH a day in my life

62044845
>>62044791
Sound like you’re just envious of a scenario that you have fabricated in your head. I manage a remote team and across our company the remote teams consistently outperform the handful of in office or hybrid teams. The truth is that once you stop limiting yourself to a physical location for hiring, the talent pool explodes.

62044878
>>62044845


In your example I'm sure you are correct. It's just that middle managers everywhere saw how much fucking off people were doing and now are convinced that WFH requires in person babysitting for a few days a week and I don't see that changing. My job pays me to travel, something WFH by definition would exclude. In my opinion that is missing out. But hey whenever makes you happy.

62045083
>>62044205
6 hours of commuting a week in total. Similar to people who commute every day 30 to 40 minutes. I think it is worth it compared to moving to the city or finding a job where I live that will pay a fraction of what I make.
>>62044791
You should be thankful how remote workers significantly reduce traffic during rush hour. I actually felt how commutes became worse as companies started demanding RTO. Fuck off you short sided seething prick.

62045322
>>62043682
I only go to the office once per month on the condition that my employer pays the plane ticket

62045520
>>62044791
And you probably never worked in a fucking office and have no clue how shitty it is to work in an open office or being surrounded by retards

62045569
>>62045083


I'm not a city slicker faggot like you lmao. My commute for the last 3 years has been 15 minutes to and from work. If you have a shitty commute, change jobs and/or move. But no, so many of you retarded faggots are absolutely against living and working outside the big city.

>>62045520

>OHH MY GOD IM WORKING INDOORS ALL DAY IM GOING INSAAAANE

I don't disagree with you, open office sounds awful. You. Picked. An. Office job lmao. You will get no sympathy from tradie wagies. Would you rather be doused in diesel fuel, ruin your back and knees, or shovel shit? Nah so please shut the fuck up. WFH is a luxury most working people will never see. And you guys let too many retards abuse it.

62045597
Fuck.
Depressing reading this thread

62045687
>>62045569
>>OHH MY GOD IM WORKING INDOORS ALL DAY
Showing your ignorance once again. Ask me how I know you've never worked an intellectual job in your life...

62045736
>>62045569
Loser pos subhuman suburban ruralfag garbage.

62045740
>>62045569
Subhuman wagie sybau

62045748
>>62045569
WE WILL NOT LET A SUBHUMAN RURALFAG BLUE COLLAR POS TALK SHIT ABOUT US

item.title: sell EVERYTHING NOW — 36 replies — 21.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
sell EVERYTHING NOW

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-03-28 20:54 UTC

OP

sell EVERYTHING NOW

Early replies (first 3)

62036605
>>62036590
That is a man, and I am very attracted to him

62036657
>>62036590
That's a woman

62036680
>>62036590

item.title: ½ Trillion Gone — 64 replies — 19.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
½ Trillion Gone

Open thread • Replies: 64 • Updated: 2026-03-28 19:14 UTC

OP

The US stock market wiped out $500 Billion dollars today. You did sell all your goyboxes, didn't you anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62036869
Haha, just kidding. It was actually a $1 Trillion dollars.

62036893
i bought the dip
panicans lose
TTP

62036894
unless you're a day trader none of this matters and even if you're a day trader still none of this matters you will lose your money regardless. fud doesn't work on proper investment strategies. longing the market won game over bye bye pink wojaks farming crypto queer

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62037311
>>62036893
Israel and Iran have their own opinions on if the war can continue.
Trump can't cuck out without their assent.

62037318
>>62036969
>1980 to 2000 was peak human civilization
Yeah I'd believe it

62037324
>>62036969
That's good news for the immortal among us here.

62037377
>>62036853
>price goes down $0.01
>SEVENTY HUNDRED BAZILLION DOLALRY DOOS WAS DELOOTED FROM DA MARKAT!
the stupidity is oh so tiresome.

62037382
>>62037377
it's the same people who want to tax unrealized gains

62037404
>>62036908
Lucky you.

62037409
>>62036935
Damn gold still up 2% ytd. I should buy more of it but I've become completely detached from all my holds.

62037465
>>62037377
It's concerning from a tax receipts perspective when your government's military and entitlement spending are 104% of receipts and $40 trillion in debt. Perhaps it is that equity movement is just noise but if the bond market breaks this little hiccup will be seen as an appetizer to the screaming. From a political perspective someone who makes the markets being strong his only feature and continues to destabilize them through retarded decisions is similarly concerning but it will cull the weak and a lot of entities caught in the crossfire. Indians are sliming each other out over gas right now, you don't realize how bad it's going to get. Even if we stopped today it could be too late. Democrats are salivating as if their ascension could do anything to alleviate the horrors. Mo money fo dem programs does not a balanced budget make. If one is secure in his employment he could do quite well but that's predicated upon all the ones that aren't getting fucked and consumed by the dying empire. It'll be hell but finally the bottom can fall out, the useless and unlucky cast aside, chuds will reign just by having access to food and shelter. The idea of 100k 1/4 ton trucks dies since no ones wages are meaningfully going up. The management class and bean counters get decimated. Today is not nothing, as a single event it would be negligible but as a part of the larger picture it's the embodiment of everything getting worse following blind optimism.

62037470
>>62037465
Americans have infinite money

62037482
>minis 1%
can't even care, wake me up with a real crash of 12-15+%

62037501
perhaps today was not the best day to try to get into trading methinks

62037510
You can always tell the /pol/faggots apart because they think that even the worst market downturn in history is some kind of spiral that will never end. They're unironically the same kind of faggots that get hyped up when a hurricane comes in and keep spamming about it when it's already gone hoping the death toll will rise. Total fucking losers lol

62037531
Whoever made this trade is probably dead now with a bullet in the head.

62037566
>>62036853
>DJIA dropped 1%
>S&P 500 dropped 1.7%
>Nasdaq-100 dropped 2.4%
OMG it's a fucking nothingburger! However can the West survive an event of this insignificance!

62037573
>>62037465
>It's concerning from a tax receipts perspective
Only if you're Dutch. Americans don't have a tax on unrealized capital gains.

62037605
>>62037510
Japanese women are so pleased with themselves!
They saved the japanese stock market with their female japanese asian women tendencies and birthrates and JAV appearances.
The yen was saved.
The japanese race is saved!

62037625
>>62037470
Its actually kind of crazy how many people don't realize this and keep acting like we should try to bring our wars in under budget. The money is infinite. It always has been infinite, it always will be infinite. There is enough money to drop a six million dollar missile on top of every individual brown person on the planet, it makes absolutely no difference.

62037699
>>62036969
Even though that chart is calculated “without inflation” it’s still affected by it.
In that there’s still a gravitational pull from scared fiat looking to park itself. So participation still increases in absolute terms.

62037786
>>62036853
I voted for this

62037819
>>62037501
It’s unironically a great day. Investing is the opposite of traffic lights. Buy red. Hold green. Sell calls and puts for premiums when it crabs. It’s really that simple. Theres going to be a lot of money made on shipping, weapons, and oil for anyone with a brain. If the market trends down invest in coffee. Its a really undersold defensive because white bitches no matter how broke will never give up their Starslops.

62038287
>>62037625
Your fucking retarded, America has crumbling infrastructure, no real manufacturing base, gutted educational standards, no major sources of economic growth accounting for inflation and a decimated middle.

The 8+ trillion plus dollars and other resources we spent on the middle east forever wars, could have easily propelled America into a global economic behemoth that dwarfs China and the rest of planet if it was well invested.

62038473
>>62036908
LMAO

62038644
>>62038287
But muh national security

62038720
>>62037293
we are also living in an era where anyone with a smartphone can be there own broker. kids growing up today are investing into markets which will keep the trend line up more than ever before. compound these facts with rampant consumerism. it's not a good time to be a bear generally speaking

62042487
>>62037625
Do you perhaps work for the Treasury Dept under the Trump administration?
You sound just retarded enough.

62042521
>>62036853
Insanely bullish for gold.

62042954
>>62036869
WHAT THE FUCK 1,000,0000,00000 TRILLONS YOU MEAN? HOW THE FUCK THE FED PRINTED SO MANY FAKE MONEY?? LMFAO

62043042
>>62036869
woooooooow so many zeroes

62043961
>>62036853

62045345
>>62037625
money is a measurement of value, and you can't print value. all this spending and inflation has yet come home to roost

item.title: Post yfw TSLA goes under $300 — 21 replies — 11.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Post yfw TSLA goes under $300

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-03-28 20:46 UTC

OP

Post yfw TSLA goes under $300

Early replies (first 3)

62040334
>>62040278
I'll slurp so much it will turn me on

62040398
>>62040278

62040438
>>62040334
Even at $200 it would be disgustingly overvalued
It's a car company and it's worth more than the next 20 biggest car companies put together and it doesn't even sell 10% of the cars.
Yes they make self driving software, and it's pretty good. You know who else does that? Every other fucking car company.
Tesla's self driving software definitely commands a slight premium over a comparable car company who sells 2,000,000 cars a year, but the premium it has is the most astronomically retarded valuation of any publicly traded company I've seen in my life. They do not and never will have a monopoly on self driving or electric cars. Those are incredibly competitive sectors in which Tesla has no greater advantage than a company like McDonald's has over Burger King or Wendy's
The valuation was based on the assumption that they'd be selling 20,000,000 cars a year someday, that number has been declining every year since 2023 and now they're discontinuing models rather than adding them.
Where on Earth is the value in this company beyond science fiction hopes and dreams of a magical fairy tale ending? What value and what expectations are being expected to justify buying Tesla at over $100 a share?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62040760
>>62040438
>car company
C'mon, don't play stupid.

62040824
>>62040438
Nice reddit post anon, didn't read

62040869
>>62040278
rolf at the tsla faggots telling me its P/E ought to be >400

62040885
>>62040760
How does it make money

62041752
>>62040278
Is this real? Do cat penises look like that?

62042422
>>62041752
if they're black they're much bigger

62042456
The dump for this stock is probably just in the beginning phase

62043093
>>62040278
It should be back to <$100

62043109
I wouldn't buy this retarded meme stock if it was $10 per share

62043159
>>62040438
Lol Tesla is the only company that makes cars that can drive themselves at any appreciable level. Nothing comes close to what a Tesla can do.

The reason it is valued so high is because the cars are so high tech and superior it is obvious to investors that they will become the predominant vehicle on the road in the future. When you drive one you realize that other car companies are dinosaurs. Tesla is so far ahead of them that they will never catch up and eventually go out of business.

Tesla is the iPhone of cars. They are revolutionary technology that is distrupting the industry. They are the only game in town as a high tech car of the future and will take over the entire market just like the iPhone did with mobile phones.

62043168
>>62043159
Thank you Grok

62043284
>>62043159
They are like iphones and needs to be replaced every 10 years.

62043299
>>62043159
This Indian GPT reply unironically makes me bullish Tesla. What everyone needs to understand is that this poster is not an anomaly, there are literally tens of millions of this very poster who unironically believes every line written in this wall of slop and will never ever sell. It's the XRP of stocks except they actually somewhat have product to parade around. Any attempted trade based on logic or numbers will simply not work when you're dealing with a cult of this magntidue and age.

Who am I kidding Elon is gonna rug the fuck out of every single one of you niggers kek

62043507
>>62043159
anon... waymos are superior. just watch this video to understand why: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kcq0tjmvGOs

62043544
>>62040278
I will try a lotto calls at 330 I think, it depends on how the war goes. I think that will likely be the all time bottom. There are so many insane crazy catalysts that can blow this up 100 bucks in a week maybe even.

1) Just end of April earnings beat, they have been reporting some good signs like beating BYD in China and Europe sales
2) Some crazy shit like Optimus Robots Fasttracked to Help War Effort
3) SpaceX Shares given 1 per 100 or so
4) Tesla Roadster really gets shown and its better than any supercar
5) Some kind of thing with Robotaxi maybe Lyft buys a fleet to compete with Uber

This is not ChatGPT I just type this way.

62043614
>>62043544
>Some crazy shit like Optimus Robots Fasttracked to Help War Effort
Do you live in a movie? Optimus isn't going to affect the war, it's a marketing product that no one will buy

62043702
>>62043507
Waymo just got caught in a lawsuit, some of their drivers are actually Filipinos from Philippine.

https://www.reuters.com/technology/waymo-defends-use-remote-assistance-workers-robotaxi-operations-2026-02-17/
https://www.youtube.com/shorts/0_t3WhPAveo

62045584
>>62043507
Waymo is a robotaxi company retard. They are only able to operate within certain cities where their models have been specifically trained like a walled garden. A Tesla is truly autonomous and can go anywhere. Even in cities with both the Tesla is still provides a more comfortable and humanlike ride.

Like I said, nobody has anything close to what Tesla can do.

item.title: Where do I put my money? — 6 replies — 6.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Where do I put my money?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-03-28 21:41 UTC

OP

As some of you already know, crypto is a scam, real-estate is in bubble, stocks are massively above their fair values. I have no interrest in any of these.

Where do I put my money to risk it all for 5-20x in 5 years???

Im all in Pokemon vintage cards right now.

BTW If you read this you will make it.

Early replies (first 3)

62044209
>Where do I put my money to risk it all for 5-20x in 5 years???
The casino.

If you are looking for actually realistic returns then stocks is probably still the best bet, despite being overvalued. Especially now that they have fallen a bit recently. Personally I would choose value stocks rather than growth or tech stocks though. And you should also keep some in cash/bonds to be prepared to buy in more if the stock market falls further.

62044233
>>62044195
>risk it all for 5-20x in 5 years
all-in on monero and bitcoin cash

62044282
>>62044195
Personally im all in funko pops and labubus. Not financial advice but dont say i didnt warn ya

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62044318
>>62044195
Antique Persian rugs

62044588
>>62044233
>monero
> bitcoin cash

trash.

62045795
>>62044195

item.title: Holy fuck I hate taxes. I barely do anything but hold and sell here and there but it took literally all day and I'm stil… — 13 replies — 5.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Holy fuck I hate taxes. I barely do anything but hold and sell here and there but it took literally all day and I'm stil…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-28 20:13 UTC

OP

Holy fuck I hate taxes.
I barely do anything but hold and sell here and there but it took literally all day and I'm still only halfway done.
Also looking at the loss I took last year was fucking painful. I turned 53ETH into 7ETH on a shitcoin. The loss will help offset some gains but fuck man unless turbotax fixed their csv input form, I'm going to have to manually enter 120 transactions.
FUCK TAXES

Early replies (first 3)

62038643
It's okay Tamaki-senpai. I can do your taxes for you

62038665
>>62038390
The funny part is knowing your taxes fund corruption. Complacency doesn't absolve you from being an accomplice, anon.

62039482
>>62038390
If you don’t live within Washington D.C. you aren’t legally required to file a 1099 Form. The same applies for states that lazily use the same form for their state tax systems. You only have to file in certain states with separate tax systems. You can also legally petition regarding your taxes, if you have grievances that are to be addressed.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62043916
I managed to finish my taxes. 10.4% effective tax rate which kinda sucks. I got in a good rhythm copying my trades watching an antis*mite stream into the STUPID FUCKING WEB INTERFACE TURBOTAX DOESN'T ACCEPT CSV FILE UPLOADS ANYMORE IT'S FUCKING RETARDED

Anyway I'm half expecting an audit because of how the IRS wanted me to file my swaps. No way to add notes explaining why I took almost $70k in losses. But they're getting money out of me anyway so maybe they won't care.

62043919
>>62038390
>give us our cut goy

62045063
>>62039482
This. Don't fall for it like the jab!!!
https://youtu.be/-11ps2MPmVA?si=GaNZweIlv1L_KIMU

62045075
>>62038390
Open turbo tax
Click import from fidelity
Send to IRS.

62045305
>>62045075
Yeah uhhhh I'm just not going to let a broker hold my assets for years and do shady shit with them I'm going to be my own bank and my keys my crypto
>not so fast -IRS
AAAAAAAIIIIIEEEEEEEE FUCKING COST BASIS AAAAAAAAGGGGHHHHH

62045342
>>62038390
kind of makes me glad I have no income tax in my state, but I get fucked on sales tax

62045358
>>62038390
>I'm going to have to manually enter 120 transactions.
I had 88k transactions in 2025 resulting in a total of 770k owed to the government even though I am actually down 500k for the year. I aggressively tax loss harvested at end of year but thanks to the new separate wallet accounting rules I accidentally sold a bunch of crypto with ultra low cost basis that I bought 3+ years ago, instead of the shit I bought 6 months ago at 5x the price it is now. Every year they make it more fake and gay to trade this stupid shit, just impose a 100% tax on it at this rate so I can on for good.

62045370
>>62043916
I actually had $3M in capital gains but $3.5M in derivatives trading losses (which is treated as income, not CG) since I got sucked into the hyperliquid/aster leveraged rape factories this year. If anyone's going to get audited it'll be me because I have these 80k trades showing I owe millions of dollars and then a single line item on my 1040 offhandedly mentioning I had a negative income of 3 million dollars this year. Luckily my service has a unofficial income report that I'll hope to attach that shows my usdc losses on these platforms line by line but it's not a real irs tax doc so probably won't do anything to stop them from flagging me for a no-lube deep probe.

62045428
>>62038665
>bro doesn't know that tax money is essentially just deleted from the economy and not actually reconstituted within state budget
you do realize that government can just print money and devalue our money that way, right? they don't NEED taxes, they could have made 0% tax and just tax us through printing more money if they wanted to
Taxes exist just to peg us softly

62045487
>>62038390
Your hard labor is funding jews who resent and despise you launching tomahawk missiles at schoolchildren and then building a social credit system surveillance prison that will disenfranchise you for having the wrong political opinion in private near a smart device.
